text.title: Backend Testing Across Languages Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Mvn test takes 4 minutes for 50 tests; each class shows 'Starting application'.
antipattern.root-cause: @SpringBootTest on every class — copied from documentation example.
antipattern.fix: Use @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) for service tests, @DataJpaTest for repositories, @WebMvcTest for controllers. Reserve @SpringBootTest for integration scenarios.

antipattern.symptom: Tests pass but the assertion never seems to run; coverage tool shows 0 hits on the assertion line.
antipattern.root-cause: #[test] over async fn; the future is never polled, body never runs.
antipattern.fix: Switch to #[tokio::test] (or runtime-specific). Add #[cfg(test)] mod tests {} grouping.

antipattern.symptom: Refactor that splits a service into two breaks 30 tests, none of which expressed real expectations.
antipattern.root-cause: Tests mocked the entire call graph 4 levels deep.
antipattern.fix: Reduce mocks to direct collaborators of the SUT. Convert deep tests into thin integration tests.

antipattern.symptom: Test passes locally, fails intermittently in CI; the SUT has a side-effect counter that increments twice.
antipattern.root-cause: assert service.do() == service.do() invoked the SUT twice in the assertion.
antipattern.fix: Always capture: result = service.do(); assert result == expected. Make side-effects observable.

antipattern.symptom: Ruby agent generates Minitest classes in an RSpec project.
antipattern.root-cause: LLM default; project context not surfaced.
antipattern.fix: Pin RSpec explicitly in the agent prompt; convert Minitest classes to describe/it blocks with factory_bot factories.
